message_body:
I've also added some more translation functions, mostly to practice the
tactics.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add `--debug-asm-symex-first` (#2036)
message_body:
This flag results in running symex on the asm side first, resulting in
easier debugging when there is an error in symbolically executing the
assembly at all.

author: Alix Trieu
message_subject: Joye double-add ladder for short Weierstrass curves in co-Z arithmetic (#1823)
message_body:
* Joye double-add ladder for short Weierstrass curves in co-Z arithmetic

* Use Z.modulo to redefine SS' as suggested by Andres and simplify proofs

* Save some loc

* More comments

* Use sigma types for the Joye ladder

* Use more sigma types and clean up

* Joye ladder: refactor SS, TT

* Simplify HordP

* Use match-goal-with to make proof more readable

* Avoid the +1-1 when defining/using SS/TT as suggested by Andres

* Refactor the ladder

---------

Co-authored-by: Alix Trieu <alix@trieu.dev>

author: Brian McSwiggen
message_subject: Fix calculation of T in mixed addition
message_body:
We were previously overwriting the values used to calculate T. This
corrects the logic, while still minimizing memory consumption. The
result is a little messier in the order of statements, but not by much.

message_body: so that toplevel invariant_proof can be accessed more quickly

author: David Benjamin
message_subject: Add a constant-time binary extended GCD algorithm.
message_body:
Thankfully, most modular inverses involve a prime or at least odd
modulus, but RSA key generation requires inverting with an even modulus
(the totient). Implementing that in constant-time is tricky.

This file adapts the binary extended GCD algorithm in the Handbook of
Applied Cryptography. This is to verify the C implementation in
https://boringssl-review.googlesource.com/c/boringssl/+/26370.

author: Samuel Tian
message_subject: refactoring

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Show more casts in PHOAS printing, sometimes (#1450)
message_body:
* Show more casts in PHOAS printing

Rather than trying to be clever about which casts to print, we instead
always print all casts when displaying PHOAS.  This can be overridden
via typeclass instances by leaving around other values for
`PHOAS.with_casts` (boolean controling the previous default) and
`PHOAS.with_all_casts` (boolean controlling the new default, overriding
`PHOAS.with_casts`).

* Only show extra casts sometimes

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Organize and fill out structures and orders (#1234)
message_body:
We're nearly at the point where we can use something like `FMapAVL` to
optimize `Assembly/Symbolic`.  (Tries would be better, but also harder.)

What remains:
1. `src/Util/FSets/FMapSect` for showing that we don't need a full
   isomorphism to port FMap structure, only an injection
2. `src/Util/FSets/FMapOption`
3. `src/Util/FSets/FMapSum`
4. `src/Util/FSets/FMapFlip` (maybe?)
5. `src/Util/FSets/FMapProd`
6. `src/Util/FSets/FMapListViaAVL` (or similar)
7. Describing the injection from `op` to something like `N * option
   AccessSize * option symbol * option N * option N`.
8. Replacing dag with mapping from `idx` to `node idx * description`,
   and mapping from `node idx` to `idx`, and an `N` holding the total
   size of the dag.
9. Updating corresponding proofs.
10. (optional) using tries instead of AVL for list-keys

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add more operations to parsing in the equivalence checker (#1197)
message_body:
We now support parsing `call`, `cmovb`, `cmp`, `db`, `dd`, `dq`, `dw`,
`je`, `jmp`, `mul`, `pop`, `push`, `rcr`, and `shrx`, and prefixes like
`rep ret`, `repz ret`, `repnz ret`.  We also support labels, `align`,
and `default rel`.  We should probably try to properly support a larger
fraction of https://www.nasm.us/xdoc/2.15.05/html/nasmdoc3.html at some
point.

We now parse instructions (opcodes) in a case-insensitive manner, though
many other places are still case-sensitive.

The stack is now computed based on push and pop instructions as well as
the adjustments to rsp, and supports aggregating multiple constant-sized
adjustments to rsp.

We don't yet support the `0F3h` syntax alternative for the hex `0xF3`,
mostly because I couldn't be bothered to write a parser for it right
now.

message_subject: Improve symbolic equivalence unification errors
message_body:
Error messages now contain explanations such as:
```
Unable to unify: [inr [191, 197, 199, 201]] == [inr [34, 40, 43, 49]]
index 0: [191, 197, 199, 201] != [34, 40, 43, 49]
index 0: 191 != 34
(add 64, [3, 8, 10, 190]) != (add 64, [3, 8, 10, 33])
index 3: 190 != 33
(and 64, [30, 31]) != (and 1, [30])
(and 64, [(selectznz, [28, 8, 29]), (const 1, [])]) != (and 1, [(selectznz, [28, 8, 29])])
Operation mismatch: and 64 != and 1
```

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add `--no-shiftr-avoid-uint1`
message_body:
We now default to avoiding uint1 in the result of `>>` operations, as
per
https://github.com/mit-plv/fiat-crypto/pull/1015#discussion_r708691741.

This is required for making symbolic equivalence checking with assembly
go through.  It might be possible to improve the symbolic equivalence
checker to know enough about bounds analysis (or to also do bounds
analysis) so that this is not necessary?

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add support for inlining functions in C
message_body:
We now support `--inline` and `--inline-internal`.

I've heard that this might increase performance of the compiled code
under gcc by about 10x for p256?

Fixes #1001

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add `ShowLevel`, rip out `with_parens` from `Show`
message_body:
Now `show` and `show_lines` no longer take a `with_parens` argument, and
instead that functionality is generalized into `ShowLevel` / `show_lvl`.
This is the first step towards having more flexibility in parenthesizing
printed expressions, in a more principled way.  It's sort-of a
test-drive of a more uniform way of level-based printing.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Pipe through an always-false equivalence checker
message_body:
This should be enough rope to check equivalence of PHOASTs with assembly
expressions.  It's plausible we want more control over the calling
conventions from the command line, for example, specifying:
- what to do when we run out of registers / how arguments are stored on
  the stack
- support for a number of common calling conventions

We may also want to emit better headers for the assembly files, rather
than replicating the header of the input file.

message_subject: Add support for interaction with files
message_body:
We now support the `--hints-file` and `--output-asm` arguments (both
unused) and the `--output` / `-o` argument.  The first two are initial
support for interacting with assembly hint files, though we don't
currently do anything with the arguments other than opening and reading
the files, and parsing the assembly.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add some basic parsing for assembly
message_body:
Also add examples from assembly files via
```bash
for i in *.asm; do (echo 'Require Import Coq.Strings.String Coq.Lists.List.'; echo 'Import ListNotations.'; echo 'Local Open Scope string_scope.'; echo 'Local Open Scope list_scope.'; echo 'Example example : list string := ['; (cat $i | grep -v "^;$(for i in $(seq 100); do echo -n .; done)" | sed 's/"/""/g; s/^\(.*\)/"\1";/g'); echo '""].') > src/Assembly/Parse/Examples/${i%.*}.v; git add  src/Assembly/Parse/Examples/${i%.*}.v; done
```

Note that we now also insert casts on the return value.  This probably
isn't required, but it seems better to do it, and I was previously not
doing it only because I didn't have easy access to the type.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Remove dependencies on the real numbers
message_body:
By copying over the implementation of nsatz, we can drop the
dependencies on the real axioms.  Together with
https://github.com/coq/coq/issues/12845, this should eliminate the last
axioms reported by coqchk, allowing us to be done with #736 (maybe
modulo adding a CI job to check that no axioms creep back in).

Not clear to me if this is worth it...

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Better computation of loose bounds
message_body:
We now compute the loose bounds by recording the number of additions
and/or balanced subtractions we want to be able to do in a row before we
need to multiply and carry.  Note that balanced subtraction always takes
a bit more overhead than addition.

This is a more conservative variant of #799 that doesn't actually change
the bounds, and makes the genuine change of #799 easier to see, namely,
reducing `headspace_add_count` from `2` to `1`.

message_subject: Update the multiret rewrite rules
message_body:
They now handle constants in adc/sbb better.  We also split the lists
and reindent a chunk of the arith_with_casts rules, in preparation for
adding a new optimization.  Doing this here will make the new
optimization more obvious.  Note that the change here doesn't actually
change any of the rules used in the C files.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Better handling of --lang, deduplicate common options
message_body:
We now parse all --lang= arguments, resulting in better error messages
when an unknown --lang is passed.  We also make it harder to add a new
common option in one place without updating all the corresponding
places.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Remove the Gallina arguments to Z_cast and Z_cast2
message_body:
This is one step closer to having cast not be a special identifier.
What remains is removing cast_outside_of_range.

Unsurprisingly, but unfortunately, this required reworking a number of
the proofs that depended on the exact structure of casts.

author: zoep
message_subject: Added Rust translation and Rust code generation API.

author: Zoe Paraskevopoulou
message_subject: Parameterized the PHOAS translation over language-specific numeric conversions and added corresponding instance in CStringification

author: Zoe Paraskevopoulou
message_subject: Moved PHOAS->IR translation out of CStringification.v

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add more heuristic definitions for unsat solinas
message_body:
We don't make use of any new ones yet, though.

Inspired by
https://github.com/mit-plv/fiat-crypto/blob/c60b1d2556a72c37f4bc7444204e9ddc0791ce4f/generate_parameters.py

I have copied the `< 2 * bitwidth` from
https://github.com/mit-plv/fiat-crypto/blob/c60b1d2556a72c37f4bc7444204e9ddc0791ce4f/generate_parameters.py#L233,
but I have no idea why we check against `2 * bitwidth` rather than
against `bitwidth`.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add CPS'd versions of arithmetic operations
message_body:
This is primarily for perf testing purposes, so we can evaluate the time
spent in the rewriter vs cbv/vm/native/etc for the cps'd versions.

We evaluate vm_compute and native_compute by parameterizing everything
over a module of runtime definitions, which can be instantiated with
module-locked opaque constants, which are not unfolded by the vm.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Reify most rewrite rules
message_body:
Currently we don't handle rules that require "concrete list of cons
cells" nor rules that require "concrete nat literal to do recursion on";
both of these require special treatment, to be implemented in an
upcoming commit.

The reifier is kind-of slow, alas.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add autogenerated docstrings to synthesized code
message_body:
We now stringify the correctness conditions to generate docstrings for
the synthesized code.

Closes #512

Time commitment: about 6 hours

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add better computation of carry chain
message_body:
We port the computation of the carry chain from generate_parameters.py
to Coq, for unsaturated solinas.

Note that while we now bounds-check p448, we do not yet support
goldilocks nor karatsuba.

However, there is still an issue with the synthesized p448 code, which
is that on 64-bit, it tries to use 256-bit and 512-bit integers.  I'm
not sure what's up with that.

Partial progress towards #507

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Switch to a more precise version of interp_related for rewrites
message_body:
This version is hopefully the right one.  It seems to be basically
exactly what we want to say, and no more and no less.  The rule for
let-ins, which is almost the only freedom we have here, is chosen to
match exactly with the rule for App+Abs.  Note that using Leibniz
equality is essential in the places where it's used.  The recursive
relation here might actually be a bit clearer as an inductive relation,
but I wanted it to simplify under reduction.

message_subject: Move chained_carries' (now chained_carries_reduce)
message_body:
It now lives in Arithmetic.Core.B.Positional, where it belongs, rather
than in Specific/.../HelperTactics.

Andres notes that we probably don't need this at all, and could instead
make chained_carries reduce after every index (and the spurious
reductions should be no-ops).  I didn't want to bother verifying this,
at the moment, so I left it as-is.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Initial stab at word-by-word montgomery
message_body:
I think I got the loop itself wrong, though:
1. I'm worried that it'll overflow off the end of the positional
   representation, since I'm not actually dividing by 2^s
2. I'm not carrying/reducing anywhere, so the getting the nth value
   might be wrong
3. I'm not sure if I got indexing right.

But I want to submit this early version to get comments, before I put
more effort into it.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Clean up ladderstep goal with help from Andres
message_body:
It's now much simpler.  Because it's much simpler, the goal that the
glue code is given has more [Tuple.map], and fewer of them unfolded, so
I've updated the glue code to handle more nested [Tuple.map] in front of
[wordToZ].

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add aborted MapCastByDeBruijnWf
message_body:
I'm not sure exactly why the wf proof requires cast_backb in map_cast;
it seems to be used as a dumb way of instantiating the
context-extension on the input expression tree (since we're only given
the context-extension values on the output expression, which has a
different type).

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add a generalized version of Barrett Reduction (#44)
message_body:
In this version, we split up the integer division so that we are less
likely to overflow in intermediate computations.

This is still not the version in HAC 14.42; that version also does early
reduction modulo b^(k+1).

This is work towards #43

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Make the library 20% faster: [auto with *] is evil
message_body:
I do hereby revoke the privilege of [intuition] to grab random hints
from random databases.  This privilege is reserved for
[debug_intuition], which comes with a warning about not being used in
production code.  This tactic is useful in conjunction with `Print Hint
*`, to discover what hint databases the hints were grabbed from.

(Suggestions for renaming [debug_intuition] welcome.)

Any file using [intuition] must [Require Export
Crypto.Util.FixCoqMistakes.].  It's possible we could lift this
restriction by compiling [FixCoqMistakes] separately, and passing along
`-require FixCoqMistakes` to Coq.  Should we do this?

author: jadep
message_subject: BaseSystem encode function is no longer naive; it does a mod/div loop rather than sticking the value of the Z input in the first digit. The condition that c is positive has been added to PseudoMersenneBaseParams--it is necessary for this encode and for canonicalization, for which it was previously a section variable.
